violations.claim: when the surrounding contract actually has at least one violation.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1 – The `console.log` of a violation line is only reachable
; when the surrounding contract actually has at least one violation.
; ------------------------------------------------------------
; Model the relevant state at the point just before line 384 is executed.
;   len            – number of violations stored in `contract.violations`
;   i              – index of the current iteration of the `for … of` loop
;   smt2Line       – a concrete line extracted from the stored SMT‑LIB text
;
; Preconditions (guaranteed by the code before the loop):
;   1. The path condition for reaching this signal is `contract.violations.length > 0`.
;   2. The loop iterates over each element, so for some index i we must have
;      0 ≤ i < len.
;
; Property to prove (P):  ∃ i . 0 ≤ i < len   (i.e. the loop body is only entered
;                                            when there is at least one element).
; To prove P we assert the negation ¬P together with the true preconditions
; and expect Z3 to report `unsat`.
;
; ------------------------------------------------------------
(declare-const len Int)          ; number of violations
(declare-const i   Int)          ; current loop index (abstract)

; --- Code guarantees (preconditions) --------------------------------
; Path condition guarantees that there is at least one violation.
(assert (> len 0))

; The loop index must be a valid index into the array.
; Negating the existence of a valid index means: for *all* i,
; either i < 0 or i ≥ len.  We model the negation directly:
; (not (and (<= 0 i) (< i len)))  ⇔  (or (< i 0) (>= i len))
; This is the condition we will assert to show unsat.
(assert (or (< i 0) (>= i len)))   ; ¬P

; ------------------------------------------------------------
(check-sat)
; Expected result: unsat
; ------------------------------------------------------------
; Interpretation:
; The only way to satisfy the code’s guarantee (> len 0) while also
; violating the existence of a valid loop index is impossible.
; Therefore the property “the loop body is entered only when there is at
; least one violation” is mathematically guaranteed.
